source.principle: Vue 3 emits `export const version = __VERSION__` in packages/runtime-core/src/index.ts. After build, the minifier interleaves the version constant with Vue 3's DOM-safety initialisation. Empirically across 3.2.37 / 3.4.0 / 3.5.0 / 3.5.13: version literal sits within ~200 chars of one of two Vue 3 distinctive DOM checks -- `"undefined"!=typeof document?document:null` (Vue 3 runtime-dom env detection, 3.2-3.4) OR `"undefined"!=typeof window&&window.trustedTypes` (Vue 3 trusted-types policy init, 3.4+). Both are stable Vue 3 internals -- the former is in packages/runtime-dom/src/index.ts, the latter in packages/runtime-dom/src/nodeOps.ts (or equivalent). The proximity anchor catches Vue 3 builds where retire.js's six v3 fingerprints fail (verified: retire.js misses 3.2.37 global entirely).
negative_tests: jquery@3.7.1 jquery.min.js -> miss (no Vue 3 trustedTypes/document check pattern)
source.complement_to_retire_js: retire.js's six v3 fingerprints are version-context-specific (SSR utils comment, __vue_app__ binding, devtoolsFormatters proximity, etc.) and miss multiple production Vue 3 builds. This rule anchors on Vue 3's DOM-safety init which is universal across the v3.x lineage.

source.principle: Vue 2 (incl. 2.7.x) emits `Object.defineProperty(Vue, 'FunctionalRenderContext', {value: FunctionalRenderContext}); Vue.version = version` consecutively in src/core/index.ts. The minifier preserves the string literal 'FunctionalRenderContext' (a defineProperty key) and the `.version=' assignment within ~200 chars. retire.js's own `Vue.version = '...'` fingerprint requires the literal prefix `Vue.version`, which minified bundles strip when the Vue constructor is renamed (e.g. `ir.version='2.7.13'`). This rule anchors on the preserved string literal instead.
source.complement_to_retire_js: retire.js fingerprint id=1124 catches `Vue.version='X.Y.Z'` literally; this rule catches the minified form where Vue is renamed.

banner_rules.applies_only_to: Vue 2.x (FunctionalRenderContext was removed in Vue 3); Vue 3 is already covered by retire.js's 9 v3-specific filecontent fingerprints.
